# UC-10.11.119 · Tanium Threat Intel Feed Application

## Description

Intel feed freshness keeps IOC hunts and blocks aligned with current campaigns.

## Value

Intel feed freshness keeps IOC hunts and blocks aligned with current campaigns.

## Implementation

Track intel feed imports into Tanium. Alert when feeds are >48h stale.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_tanium`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=tanium:intel`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Track intel feed imports into Tanium. Alert when feeds are >48h stale.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype="tanium:intel"
| stats latest(import_time) as it by feed_name
| where now()-it > 172800
```

Understanding this SPL

**Tanium Threat Intel Feed Application** — Intel feed freshness keeps IOC hunts and blocks aligned with current campaigns.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=tanium:intel`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_tanium`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: endpoint; **sourcetype**: tanium:intel. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=endpoint, sourcetype="tanium:intel". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by feed_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where now()-it > 172800` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendor’s own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (stale feeds), Single value.

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype="tanium:intel"
| stats latest(import_time) as it by feed_name
| where now()-it > 172800
```
